channel.title: Accord Protocol Learn
channel.description: Guides to agent work agreements, verification receipts, settlement receipts and Accord Protocol architecture.

item.title: What Are Agent Work Agreements?
item.description: A practical explanation of agent work agreements: terms, authority, verification and settlement records for autonomous software work.

item.title: Accord Protocol vs x402
item.description: How Accord and x402 fit together: x402 verifies paid HTTP access, while Accord verifies completion of the work around that payment.

item.title: Verification Receipts for AI Agents
item.description: Why verification receipts matter for autonomous agent workflows and how they record accepted, rejected or partially accepted work.

item.title: Settlement Receipts and Agent Payment Records
item.description: How Settlement Receipts record the economic closeout of agent work across rails without making Accord a payment processor.

item.title: Accord/MCP for Paid and Verifiable Tools
item.description: How Accord/MCP adds payment and completion semantics around MCP tools used by autonomous agents.

item.title: Rail Adapters in Accord Protocol
item.description: A guide to Accord rail adapters and how they separate agreement semantics from settlement mechanisms.

item.title: How to Become an Accord-Compatible Provider
item.description: A practical onboarding path for APIs, MCP tools and agent services that want to emit Accord agreements, verification receipts and settlement receipts.

item.title: Accord Launch Readiness Gates
item.description: Why Accord is holding broad public launch until the evidence, provider onboarding and audit posture are polished.

item.title: Why Ergo Is the First Accord Reference Rail
item.description: Why Accord uses Ergo as its first reference programmable settlement rail, and what claims should be avoided until audits are complete.

item.title: Accord Conformance Levels L0-L4
item.description: What Accord conformance levels mean, what they test and why conformance is not the same as production certification.

item.title: Audit-Gated Mainnet Policy for Accord
item.description: Why Accord keeps mainnet use blocked by default until signed audit manifests explicitly allow relevant scripts or contracts.

item.title: Accord Protocol vs AP2
item.description: How Accord relates to AP2-style authorization: AP2 helps express authority, while Accord records work terms, verification and settlement.

item.title: Accord Protocol vs MCP
item.description: MCP connects agents to tools. Accord adds paid-work terms, verification receipts and settlement records around tool calls.

item.title: How Accord/402 Works
item.description: A step-by-step guide to Accord/402, the HTTP paid-resource flow that combines payment proof with agreement and verification receipts.

item.title: How to Run the Paid MCP Repository Audit Demo
item.description: A practical walkthrough of the safest first Accord demo: the mock-rail paid MCP repository audit lifecycle.

item.title: The Accord Agreement Object Explained
item.description: A plain-language guide to the Accord Agreement Object and the fields that define paid agent work.

item.title: Buyer Policy for Agent Wallets
item.description: How buyer-side policy can limit agent spend and connect payment authority to Accord agreements and receipts.

item.title: What Accord Conformance Does Not Prove
item.description: A safety-focused explanation of the limits of conformance testing and why compatibility is not production certification.

item.title: Live Testnet Evidence: Accord Base Sepolia Contract Rail
item.description: Accord completed a live Base Sepolia contract rail pilot with deployed contract evidence, testnet transactions, audit-gate checks and L4 conformance.

item.title: The Agent Payments Stack: MCP, x402, AP2 and Accord
item.description: A practical map of the agent payments stack: MCP for tool access, x402 for HTTP payment, AP2 for payment authority and Accord for work verification.

item.title: Payment Verification Is Not Work Verification
item.description: Why paid AI agent systems need separate records for payment, promised work, verifier decisions and settlement evidence.

item.title: Production Checklist for Paid MCP Tools
item.description: A builder checklist for paid MCP tools covering agreements, pricing, buyer policy, verifier design, receipts, replay protection and audit gates.

item.title: How to Design Verifiers for Paid Agent Work
item.description: A practical guide to verifier design for paid AI agent workflows: deterministic tests, human review, model graders, committees and receipt boundaries.

item.title: Agent Wallet Policy: How to Keep Autonomous Spend Bounded
item.description: A guide to buyer-side policy for agent wallets: spend caps, recipient allow-lists, rails, approvals, signer isolation and receipt-aware settlement.

item.title: Conformance, Audits and Mainnet Gates for Agent Payment Rails
item.description: How to separate Accord compatibility, external audits, rail risk, verifier assumptions and mainnet readiness for agent payment systems.

item.title: The Agent Receipt Layer for Autonomous Work
item.description: How Accord combines agreement, verification receipt and settlement receipt records so paid AI work can be inspected after execution.

item.title: How x402 and Accord Fit Together
item.description: x402 can unlock paid HTTP resources; Accord adds work agreements, verifier receipts and settlement records around the paid task.

item.title: Replay Protection for Paid MCP Tools
item.description: How to prevent payment proofs, tool calls and receipts from being reused across sessions, providers or rails.

item.title: Verifier Design Patterns for Agent Receipts
item.description: Patterns for deterministic verifiers, model graders, human review, committees and hybrid verification in Accord workflows.

item.title: Audit Manifests Explained
item.description: How Accord uses signed manifests, exact artifact identity and default-deny mainnet gates instead of broad audit badges.
